text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, to make sure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests go a step further by verifying that different parts of the system work together properly. Many developers use test-driven development, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps catch problems early and guides the design of the code. Together, these testing practices build confidence. When you run a full suite of tests and they all pass, you can feel sure that new changes won't break existing features. This makes it easier to improve software safely over time.
